content:
📸 Live from the floor at HousingWire's The Gathering 📸 : Allison LaForgia sat down with our CEO Diane Yu and Corey Shelley at First Colony Mortgage, one of Tidalwave's earliest customers, to talk about what it actually takes to put AI on a live loan file.

First Colony Mortgage has been using Tidalwave for about a year, starting in wholesale and now running both wholesale and retail on our agentic AI platform. Together we've been attacking one of the ugliest jobs in lending: reviewing 700-page broker packages by hand.

A few things from Corey worth pulling out:

▶️ On why they brought us in: "With wholesale, we were growing so fast, and margins are thin and scalability is hard. What we saw with Diane's product is a way that we could scale, cut out a lot of time, without having to keep hiring and hiring more people."

▶️ On what actually changed by using Tidalwave: "Wholesale loan packages are 500 to 1000 pages. We call it 'stare and compare.' Tidalwave took those 1000 pages, immediately organizes them for us. You kind of have to break how you think about mortgages. Underwriting starts the second the borrower uploads everything."

▶️ On what his team is saying: "My loan officers love it. I'm getting messages and texts constantly saying, 'Hey, this is the greatest thing that we've ever used.' My underwriter is saying we're saving so much time. The AI is showing us everything we need to see immediately."

The numbers behind those quotes: First Colony Mortgage scaled wholesale volume from near-zero to $600M in about 18 months on Tidalwave. Not by adding headcount. By rebuilding the workflow. They're now our first customer running both sides of the business on the platform.

Worth a watch if you're trying to figure out where AI in mortgage is actually delivering measurable impact.
Take a listen to our own Chris McLendon!

Underwriters spend two to four hours per borrower manually reviewing bank statements. Categorizing transactions. Flagging recurring payments. Hunting for invisible debts. 

❌ Every hour of delay is an hour a borrower spends considering a faster lender. 

✅ Tidalwave's Asset Assist completes the same forensic analysis in five to six minutes. It categorizes every transaction, identifies income patterns, and flags invisible debts, recurring deposits, and potential rent payments. The full breakdown is ready when the underwriter opens the file.

Lenders, try a demo and see how Tidalwave runs on a real file: tidalwave.com/book-a-call

Tidalwave brought together some remote Tech and Engineering teams this week to collaborate in NYC.

Appreciate ICE Mortgage Technology for the opportunity to tour the NYSE while we had everyone in town. Great experience for the team.

Yesterday we hosted our first Flying SOLO, Life After Launch session and the energy was infectious! 

70+ clients joined us for a real conversation on Best Practices for Loan Officers. Practical ways to create cleaner files earlier, reduce friction, and keep things moving seamlessly.

What stood out most was the engagement. Honest questions, shared experiences, and a willingness to embrace all things SOLO! 

We’re just getting started and already looking forward to the next one.

#FlyingSolo

Five of us here at Tidalwave are Austin-bound for HousingWire's The Gathering next week. 

There's a reason this is the conference people prioritize. HousingWire consistently puts the right leaders in the room: lenders, brokers, servicers, investors, policymakers, and the tech teams building alongside them. The agenda always matches the moment, and this year's moment is a big one.

Tidalwave's Diane Yu, Chris McLendon, John Stephenson, Alicia Goncalves, CMB®, and Christopher Olsen will be on the ground all week. If you want to talk about what agentic AI looks like in production for mortgage lenders, or just want to grab coffee on site, reach out.

See you in Austin!

A 2025 report found that 11.5% of US mortgage loan file content has contained errors or missing information over the past decade. That translates to an estimated $7.8 billion in increased costs for consumers.

Bad data enters the pipeline. Weeks later, someone cleans it up.

Tidalwave's Document Assist agent reviews every upload in real time. It extracts data with 98%+ accuracy using mortgage-specific knowledge, catches name variations and typos with smart matching, flags mismatches across documents, and rejects files that don't meet quality standards at the source.

Bad data never enters the system.

Lenders:, see how it works: tidalwave.com/book-a-call

❌ Lenders lose most of their mortgage leads in the first 24 hours. Every lead costs money to generate. Every one that goes cold is a full loss, and the lender who responds first gets the loan.

Tidalwave's voice-to-AI technology closes that window. It turns phone conversations into started mortgage applications in real time, extracting loan data from the call and populating the application as the borrower talks.

✅ Here's what it replaces. A borrower calls after work and gets sent to voicemail. They fill out a web form that asks them to repeat everything they just said. They wait overnight for a callback. By morning, another lender has already sent them a pre-qual letter.

✅ With Tidalwave, there's no callback delay. No repeat intake form. Our agentic AI answers borrower questions 24/7 in plain language, and voice-to-AI captures the application the moment the borrower picks up the phone.

✅ The result: a 40% reduction in lead abandonment in the first 24 hours.

Lenders: see it in a live walkthrough → tidalwave.com/book-a-call

Most mortgage delays aren't underwriting problems. They're data problems that show up at underwriting.

❌ A name that doesn't match across documents. A deposit that wasn't explained at application. An inconsistency no one caught until a processor three weeks in flagged it as a condition.

By then, the borrower is frustrated, the loan officer is scrambling, and another week is added to the timeline.

Tidalwave's agentic AI, SOLO, catches those problems at entry. Mortgage-specific extraction at 98%+ accuracy. Name mismatches, document inconsistencies, and data gaps flagged before they ever reach a processor.

✅ A file that arrives at underwriting clean moves differently than one that doesn't. That's how processing time drops from 43 days to under two weeks.

Lenders: book a demo to see what a clean pipeline looks like from day one. https://lnkd.in/eZuZfdR2

A borrower who speaks limited English has a 1 in 3 chance of abandoning their mortgage application. Not because they can't afford the home. Because the process was never built for them.

23% of Hispanic borrowers cite language as a barrier. Only 17% received any translation support from their lender. Close to 5 million U.S. households have limited English proficiency, and most mortgage platforms still treat that as someone else's problem.

Tidalwave's agentic AI, SOLO, provides full mortgage guidance in over 30 languages. Not a translated PDF. Not a Spanish-speaking staff member who may or may not be available. Real-time explanation of mortgage terminology, specific to that borrower's situation, at any hour.

For lenders, that's a market segment your current platform is likely losing. You don't need to hire to serve it. Book a demo to see how Tidalwave works: https://lnkd.in/eZuZfdR2

If you've ever applied for a mortgage, you know the application alone can feel like a second job. 

Dig up two years of tax returns. Email your bank statements. Wait. Get asked for the same bank statements in a different format. Wait again. Wonder if anyone is actually looking at your file.

Lenders using Tidalwave's agentic AI, SOLO, give their borrowers a different experience. Borrowers apply via web, mobile, text, or phone. SOLO extracts their information, runs automated underwriting through Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac simultaneously, verifies documents as they're uploaded, and builds a personalized task list around their actual financial profile. Fully verified. 
All documents uploaded. 35 minutes.

The 43-day average close time doesn't start with underwriting. It starts at the application. That's where we fixed it.

Book a demo to see what your borrowers' experience could look like. https://lnkd.in/eZuZfdR2

At Experience, we talked a lot about meaningful conversations.

Not quick badge scans. Not surface level intros. Real conversations about what people are building, where they are headed, and how we can help.

And because people showed up for that in a big way, something pretty incredible came out of it.

Through our Wave Passes, we were able to give back to two amazing organizations, Habitat for Humanity and Homes For Our Troops.

That only happens because attendees leaned in, took the time, and chose to engage with intention. We have always believed connection should create impact. This was a great example of that coming to life.

To everyone who stopped by, had a conversation, and participated, thank you. You made this bigger than just another event.
We are excited to keep the conversation going and share more about what we are building at Tidalwave soon.

Until then, just know you made more than connections at EXP this year!

🗞️ Inside Mortgage Finance Publications' Shannon Clark just covered  Tidalwave and Columbia Data Agents Process Lab (DAPLab)'s 
benchmark study measuring AI accuracy on real mortgage origination tasks and it's worth a read.

➡️ Researchers at Columbia Data Agents Process Lab (DAPLab) built 90 questions across 10 borrower scenarios, testing Tidalwave's SOLO against Anthropic's Claude 4.5 on the exact questions loan officers ask during origination: Does the payroll match the stated employer? Are there undisclosed liabilities? Could a deposit be from a foreign source?

➡️ The results: Tidalwave's AI agent scored 84% overall vs. 71% for the general-purpose model. On compliance checks, where wrong answers lead to bad loans and audit failures, Tidalwave scored 95%. Anthropic's Claude scored 42%.

Tidalwave CEO Diane Yu says this "a 42% accuracy number should worry every lender using 'off the shelf' AI. If you're going to tell lenders your AI is accurate, you should be willing to prove it publicly."

First Colony Mortgage just fully launched both the wholesale and retail function on Tidalwave, so of course the first order of business was to grill our Head of Customer Success, Christopher Olsen, over hot wings.

🔥 In this Burning Questions episode, Brandon Christensen hosts a 30‑minute roundtable with First Colony Mortgage’s Chief Technology Product Officer Nick Christensen and Chris, where the sauces get hotter and the questions get sharper.

They cover what Tidalwave actually takes off a loan officer’s plate, why agentic AI is closer to “book the whole vacation” than “answer a travel question,” how AI can catch closing and funding errors before loans get stuck or unsellable, and why First Colony Mortgage is bullish on agentic AI.

Catch Brandon Christensen, Nick Christensen, and Christopher Olsen talk all things AI and very spicy wings here:

Tidalwave’s CEO Diane Yu spoke with HousingWire about the Trump administration's proposed AI policy framework and what it means for mortgage lenders navigating compliance across 50 states. The proposal pushes for a single federal standard instead of state-by-state regulation.

The question: Does that accelerate AI adoption or add more friction?

Diane’s POV: lenders in all 50 states are stuck in legal review because compliance teams cannot interpret fragmented AI laws, even though the industry already runs on one national set of underwriting guidelines through Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac. Adding a second patchwork of state AI rules does not create clarity, it creates chaos.

Curious how a federal AI standard could change lender timelines and costs? Read more:
https://lnkd.in/eryPngnZ

🗞️ HousingWire’s Sarah Wolak just broke an exclusive on a new benchmark study from Tidalwave and Columbia Data Agents Process Lab (DAPLab).

In the study, researchers from Columbia Data Agents Process Lab (DAPLab) tested Tidalwave’s AI agent SOLO, against Anthropic’s Claude 4.5 on 90 real mortgage questions across 10 borrower scenarios:

Does the payroll match the stated employer? Are there buy‑now‑pay‑later payments? Could any deposits be from a foreign source? Is there undisclosed income?

Here’s what they found:

➡️ Overall accuracy: Tidalwave’s SOLO scored 84%, compared to 71% for Anthropic's Claude 4.5.

✅ On yes/no compliance checks (payroll matching, BNPL detection, undisclosed income), Tidalwave's SOLO hit 95% accuracy, while Anthropic's Claude 4.5 scored 42%.

❌  At 42%, a general‑purpose model gets these compliance questions wrong more often than right. If you’re buying a home, those are the checks that catch problems before they become your problems.

The benchmark was spearheaded by Tidalwave co‑founders Cheng Li and Diane Yu, built with Columbia Data Agents Process Lab (DAPLab)’s, and grounded in the questions loan officers actually ask every day.

We published the full results, including the one category where Tidalwave's SOLO scored lower, because lenders deserve data, not marketing.

Read the full story in HousingWire:
https://lnkd.in/e5UJ8PZ6

As we prepare for ICE Experience, we also want to take a moment to recognize the leadership behind our organization.

Our CEO, Diane Yu has built her career at the intersection of innovation, technology, and client impact.

Her vision continues to shape how we approach product development, partnerships, and the way we show up for our clients.

In an industry where representation in technology leadership matters, we are proud to spotlight a leader who is driving meaningful change.

If you’ll be at ICE Experience, Diane and team would love to connect and share more about the direction we’re headed.
#OnTheRoadWithTidalwave #ICEEXP26 #ConnectionsThatMatter

Here are a few we hear all the time:

At conferences, the best conversations happen when we can go beyond the surface and talk through real workflows, real challenges, and real goals.

If you’re attending ICE Experience, stop by our booth, #209, and bring your toughest questions. We’re ready for them.

Because meaningful conversations lead to meaningful solutions!
#OnTheRoadWithTidalwave #ICEEXP26 #ConnectionsThatMatter

We are handing out Wave Passes to every person who has a meaningful conversation with our team. Not a quick badge scan. Not a rushed hello. A real conversation about what your team is working toward.

And here is where it gets even better.

For every demo booked at the conference, we will donate to one of three pre-selected charities. You choose the cause. We make the contribution.
Because connection should create impact.

Stop by our booth, 209, grab a Wave Pass, and let’s have a conversation that matters!

#OnTheRoadWithTidalwave #ICEEXP26 #ConnectionsThatMatter

Our full team will be on site talking about how lenders are simplifying workflows, improving borrower experiences, and getting more value from their technology stack.
If you are attending, we still have time slots open for meetings.

Come by our booth or book time here https://hubs.ly/Q044hxQW0

Whether you want a deep dive conversation or a quick hello between sessions, we would love to connect.

#OnTheRoadWithTidalwave #ICEEXP26 #ConnectionsThatMatter

We are proud to be a Gold Sponsor, and can’t wait to connect with lenders, partners, and friends across the industry.

Cat's out of the bag! 🗞️ 

HousingWire's Neil Pierson broke the news today: Flat Branch Home Loans is the first independent mortgage bank to go live with Tidalwave's agentic AI platform on an end-to-end basis.

We've been heads down on this one for a while. And the early results speak for themselves.

➡️ Flat Branch Home Loans's new point-of-sale system, Flat Branch Home, has converted 90% of consumer applications to submissions. In an industry where borrowers routinely abandon apps the moment they hit paperwork walls and manual follow-ups, that number matters.

➡️ This is what happens when a forward-thinking lender decides to rebuild the borrower and loan officer experience from the ground up, not just bolt on another tool.

Huge credit to Jamie Pandolfo, Michael Eckley, Taylor Nedrow, Elena Steinmetz and the entire Flat Branch Home Loans team for leaning into this. Partners like this are how you move an industry.

Read more in HousingWire: https://lnkd.in/eu8Wzdhd

📉 Mortgage rates hit a three-year low today.

✅ For borrowers, that's the green light they've been waiting for. Every rate drop unlocks a new pool of buyers, people who couldn't qualify at 7% who can now qualify at 6%. When rates dipped to 6.16% last month, applications jumped 28.5% in one week. Refinances up 40%. Purchase activity up 30%. Two years of pent-up demand, finally moving.

For lenders, it's a different story.

✅ Volume surges mean phones ringing, pipelines flooding, and a 43-day closing process that can't keep up. The response is always the same: hire fast. Then rates tick up, volume drops, and layoffs follow. The average lender already loses ~$600 per loan  and the boom-bust staffing cycle makes the math worse.

With Tidalwave, lenders don’t have to ride that hiring/firing rollercoaster. By automating up to 70% of the mortgage workflow, Tidalwave lets teams absorb demand without scrambling to staff up, then cutting back when the market turns.​

So as rates move, the question isn’t “how fast can we hire?”
It’s “why are we still using headcount to paper over manual work?”

See how it works: https://lnkd.in/evFyU-FP

🌊🏆 Big News: Tidalwave has been named a 2026 HousingWire Tech100 Mortgage winner! 🌊🏆

➡️The HousingWire Tech100 is usually a roll call of the industry’s biggest players. Seeing a startup like ours on that list says a lot. We’re honored to be recognized alongside established leaders like Freddie Mac and ICE Mortgage Technology, as well as innovators like Cotality, Lodestar, Argyle, and Informative Research.

The industry’s done digitizing PDFs, it’s time for agentic AI that actually drives the loan from start to finish.

Read more about why Tidalwave won  👇🏽 
https://lnkd.in/e8KUT8d6

Our team spent last week at Fairway Independent Mortgage Corporation's AM26 conference in Dallas with over 1,200 loan officers, branch managers, LOAs, ops leaders, and executives.

➡️ Tidalwave CEO Diane Yu Yu joined Fairway Independent Mortgage Corporation's Bryan Moran on stage for a fireside chat, sharing her background and the journey behind building Tidalwave.

➡️ Diane also took the stage with Fairway Independent Mortgage Corporation leaders Mike Rogers and Kelly Rogers to discuss how Tidalwave's agentic AI is reshaping mortgage origination, from speed and accuracy to a better experience for lenders and borrowers alike.

➡️ The questions afterward weren't about whether AI works - they were about implementation timelines, integration specifics, and cost savings per loan.
The conference themes resonated: "promises made, promises kept" and "one more loan." Lenders are done with platforms that overpromise. They want automation that closes loans faster with the team they have, not tools that add more steps.

It was great to catch up with our partners Argyle, Informative Research, Snapdocs, Arch Mortgage Insurance Company (Arch MI), and Total Expert who are all pushing this industry forward.

Thanks to the Fairway Independent Mortgage Corporation team for putting together another great event!

Christopher Olsen Chris McLendon Diane Yu John Stephenson

Fortune's Allie Garfinkle included Tidalwave CEO Diane Yu Yu's perspective in today's Term Sheet on what 2026 holds for the future of work. 📰 

Diane's POV: agentic AI is moving from copilots to autonomous operators, with systems that handle complete workflows, not just assist with tasks. 

➡️ The shift she described - from copilots to autonomous operators - is already playing out in mortgage. What that looks like at Tidalwave: AI that handles mortgage verification through Plaid, Argyle, and Truv, delivers instant underwriting via Fannie Mae Mae and Freddie Mac. Document collection and compliance without human intervention.

➡️ Lenders like NEXA Lending, First Colony Mortgage, Mortgage Solutions Financial, and D.R. Horton are using Tidalwave's agentic AI to automate complete workflows with minimal human oversight.

Read Diane's full perspective in Fortune: https://lnkd.in/eQPvcD6t

"At Tidalwave, we believe in 'shifting left'—getting to a clean, high-confidence application the moment we connect with a borrower," said Diane Yu, CEO of Tidalwave. "Truv gives us reliable, real-time data that lets our AI assistant go to work earlier and with greater certainty."

📰 HousingWire’s Flávia Furlan Nunes reported Tidalwave is targeting 200,000 loans annually by 2026. That's roughly 4% of the projected U.S. mortgage market.

➡️ Here's what that means in practice: Freddie Mac research shows digital automation cuts origination costs by $1,500 per loan. We're not replacing loan officers. We're eliminating the manual tasks that consume most of their time so they can focus on borrowers.

➡️ The average mortgage still takes 43 days to close. That timeline hasn't changed in decades. It should. More on Tidalwave in HousingWire 👇🏽 https://lnkd.in/eXev8qD4

Fortune's Allie Garfinkle asked Tidalwave CEO Diane Yu a simple question: why does the average mortgage still take 43 days to close? 

The answer: hundreds of manual tasks across disconnected systems. Borrowers submit their entire financial history, then wait in the dark for six weeks to find out if they're approved. It's mind-blowing that the biggest financial transaction most of us will make in our lifetimes is so antiquated and confusing.

Allie’s piece below walks through how our direct integrations with Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac automates the evaluation of mortgage documents, and provides real-time, multilingual feedback to borrowers. The goal isn't just speed. It's transparency. Borrowers deserve to know where they stand.

More on Tidalwave in Fortune: https://lnkd.in/eJzDbpmV

"The tide is moving and this time, the current has code behind it." Jesse Landry Thank you for amplifying our voice! Appreciate it. 

#AI #AgenticAI #Startup #Fintech #MortgageTech #Mortgage

🎉 Big news! Tidalwave has raised $22M in Series A funding led by Permanent Capital Partners, with participation from D.R. Horton and Engineering Capital.

➡️ The mortgage industry processes 6.5 million loans annually in the U.S. Each one takes an average of 43 days and requires hundreds of manual tasks across disconnected systems. Borrowers wait weeks for decisions that should take minutes. Lenders spend thousands per loan on inefficient processes.

➡️ Tidalwave uses agentic AI to automate complete mortgage tasks, verification, underwriting, document collection, with minimal human oversight.

➡️ With this funding, we're on track to power 200,000+ loans annually, approximately 4% of all U.S. mortgage originations.

➡️ D.R. Horton, the nation's largest homebuilder, evaluated every company in this space before choosing to become both an investor and enterprise customer.

Read the full story by Allie Garfinkle in Fortune:https://lnkd.in/eJzDbpmV

📰 News alert! HousingWire interviewed our founder and CEO Diane Yu together with our new COO Jason Cao about Jason's decision to move from early investor to full-time executive at Tidalwave.

Jason backed Tidalwave before we even had a product, and now he is here full time helping run day-to-day operations and finance. 

He joins a growing team of mortgage technology leaders who recently came aboard, including Chris McLendon, John Stephenson, Frank Pastirchak, Shawna Adams-Johnson, Ivón Vazquez, and Shelby Huster.

Big thanks to Sarah Wolak and the HousingWire team for telling the story behind the team we’re building. 

Article below 👇🏽

Register in comments 👇🏽

We’re excited to announce that Jason Cao has joined Tidalwave as our Chief Operating Officer. 

Jason was one of our first believers and he invested early when most people were still skeptical that AI could truly automate mortgage origination. He saw what we were building and backed the team.

Now he's joining us to help scale what we started.

Jason comes from CertiK, where as COO he helped grow the company into the world's largest blockchain security company and a unicorn. His track record in ​building and scaling is exactly what we need as we grow.

From early investor to full-time ​b​uilder. Excited to have you on the team, Jason Cao!

🏆 Big news: Tidalwave’s own Stephanie Selig has been named a 2025 HousingWire #HousingWireTechTrendsetters honoree.🏆

Stephanie was one of our earliest hires and brings 25+ years of mortgage operations expertise to our AI platform. She’s the driver behind critical innovations, including our seamless integration with Fannie Mae Mae and Freddie Mac that help lenders move faster, cut down origination headaches, and bring more families affordable paths to homeownership.

Stephanie doesn’t just automate workflows; she turns the “unwritten rules” of underwriting into real, scalable features our clients trust every day. Her leadership is felt in every loan file our system touches.

Congratulations, Stephanie! Your impact has directly improved how lenders work and ultimately, how borrowers access homeownership. 

Please join us in celebrating her well-deserved recognition! More on the award here: https://lnkd.in/eNx3u-p3

📉 Yesterday's Fed rate cut triggered the cycle mortgage lenders know too well: rates drop, applications surge, teams scramble to hire, then layoffs follow when demand cools. It's been the industry playbook for years, but in the era of AI, it doesn't have to be. ❌ 

Tidalwave's automation changes the equation. Instead of staffing up and cutting back, lenders can handle end-to-end origination from pre-approvals, underwriting and compliance through agentic AI. With integrations to Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Plaid, Argyle, and Truv, real-time verification and instant decisions become standard, not exceptions. Lenders using Tidalwave gain:

✅ Instant AI-powered loan approvals
✅ Automated tasks freeing loan officers and underwriters
✅ Spanish-language support to reach more borrowers
✅ Demand adaptation without workforce chaos

As rates continue shifting and borrower demand stays unpredictable, Tidalwave gives lenders the flexibility and efficiency they need to stay competitive. Curious how it works? Schedule a demo:  https://lnkd.in/evFyU-FP

🏆 Big news: Tidalwave was named a winner of the 2025 Inman AI Awards, recognizing AI-driven solutions reshaping how real estate is bought, sold, and financed!  🏆 

We’re honored to be included among companies and practitioners pushing the industry forward. As Inman CEO Emily Paquette put it, this year’s winners “represent the cutting edge of innovation,” enhancing efficiency, elevating customer experience, and opening new possibilities for the industry.

At Tidalwave, that looks like agentic workflows that automate the heavy lift in mortgage origination, speeding cycle times, reducing back-and-forth, and making the borrower journey clearer and more transparent. 

Thank you to Inman for the recognition and to our partners who build with us every day!

Full list of winners: https://lnkd.in/gQPZGy6n

📸 Just spotted at the #MBAAnnual25: Diane Yu, CEO and co-founder of Tidalwave catching up with Heidi Patalano, editor-in-chief of National Mortgage News.

Two forces in mortgage tech bringing festive energy (holiday colors and all) while swapping insights on AI-driven mortgage lending and what’s next for the industry.

Who says October is too early to be festive and merry? 🎄

Vegas, here we come! Excited to be heading to Mortgage Bankers Association Annual 2025 next week with the Tidalwave team. If you’ll be there, let’s connect! Would love to chat about how Tidalwave’s agentic AI is helping lenders move faster and close more loans. We’ll be doing live demos throughout the week - DM me or email diane@tidalwave.com if you want to meet up!

Our team is heading to Las Vegas for the Mortgage Bankers Association Annual 2025 next week! 🚀 

We’re looking forward to connecting with mortgage leaders and innovators shaping the future of lending. We’ll be offering live demos on how Tidalwave’s agentic AI POS is helping lenders reduce costs, improve accessibility, and accelerate approvals.

If you’ll be there, drop a comment or find us on the floor to connect.
#MBAAnnual2025

Mortgage rates just fell to a 6-month low. 📉 While that’s great news for borrowers, mortgage lenders are bracing for a flood of refi applications as homeowners scramble to lock in cheaper payments.

➡️ Here’s what actually happens: When rates drop, refi applications spike. Lending teams get buried under a mountain of manual work, with 45-day closings, giant files, endless paper chasing, and phone tag. 

➡️ When volume surges, these headaches multiply.

➡️ This is the moment Tidalwave’s agentic AI was designed for. Our platform cuts out the busywork, automating core tasks and replacing paperwork with direct digital checks through Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, Plaid and others. Lenders get verifications in seconds, not days.

🤔 Want to see how it works and how fast it can be? Schedule a Tidalwave demo:

🏆 LIVE from #DIGMOEvent at Digital Mortgage 2025! 🏆 

Lights, camera, action! Tidalwave's Diane Yu + Christopher Olsen took the stage at the National Mortgage News Digital Mortgage 2025 Demo Challenge, delivering a live demo of how Tidalwave’s AI platform simplifies and accelerates the mortgage process to a packed room of industry peers.

A huge thank you to real-time judges: Jonathan Kunkle (Freddie Mac), John Wines (Atlantic Bay Mortgage Group), and Julian Hebron (The Basis Point®) Will Adams (LendingTree) for evaluating each pitch and sharing their industry insight. Judges scored demos on these five essentials:

📈Market Potential: How game-changing is the product for lenders?
🏅Likelihood of Success: Team strength, customer traction, stability.
💡Innovation: Does it actually solve real-world issues in new—and effective—ways?
🖥️User Experience: Is it intuitive and compelling?
 🎤Demo & Showmanship: Did the product deliver live?

🚨 BREAKING: Just announced at HousingWire's AI Summit—Tidalwave is partnering with NEXA Mortgage, the nation's largest mortgage broker! When the country's largest broker puts AI in the hands of all 3,200+ of its loan officers, the entire industry changes forever.  🚨

📝  For decades, mortgages meant faxes, paper, weeks of delays, and frustrated borrowers. Most of the industry still runs on outdated, manual systems. Today, that changes.

With Tidalwave’s AI:
➡️ Processing drops from a month and a half to just hours
➡️ Loan origination costs shrink by $1,500+ per loan
➡️ Up to 70% of repetitive admin work vanishes
➡️ Loan officers can finally focus on helping families—no more chasing paperwork

🤔 How? Our platform connects directly to Fannie Mae DU and Freddie Mac LPA for real-time underwriting. It instantly verifies income, assets, and employment with Plaid, Argyle, and Truv—eliminating errors and delays. And we’ve built in Spanish-language support, meeting the needs of the 23% of Hispanic borrowers who often face language barriers.

The result:
▶️ Less stress for borrowers and loan officers
▶️ Faster loan decisions and closings
▶️ More families served, more dreams realized

When the largest broker in the country goes all-in on AI, the rest of the industry has to catch up.

Thank you to Mike Kortas + Jason duPont at NEXA Mortgage for leading this shift, and Sarah Wheeler and HousingWire for the exclusive coverage. 📰

It’s 2025. We’ve “Uberized” every single industry—except mortgage. How is it possible that the most significant financial decision most people make still involves 500-page files, 45-day delays, and lenders hemorrhaging $645 per loan on manual processes? Here’s how we are using AI to help lenders and mortgage seekers alike: 

▶️ Gives immediate pre-qualification: TidalWave’s SOLO™ integrates with Freddie Mac's LPA℠ and Fannie Mae’s DU® systems allows lenders to assess loan risks instantly, speeding up pre-qualifications have been underwriting tested.

▶️ Reduces costs for lenders through automation: The average loan origination cost exceeds $10,000, with lenders losing $645 per loan due to inefficiencies. TidalWave automates manual tasks like income verification and compliance checks, significantly lowering costs.

▶️ Task automation for loan officers and underwriters: TidalWave’s SOLO™ uses Plaid and AccountCheck to validate income, employment checks, and tax form transcription, freeing loan officers from mundane tasks and allowing them to focus on closing more loans

▶️ AI multilingual support: Nearly 23% of Hispanic Americans face language barriers during the mortgage process, with 31% considering abandoning their applications. TidalWave’s Spanish-language integration guides borrowers through the process in their native language, reducing misunderstandings and costs associated with hiring translators.

The result? Lenders regain profitability. Borrowers actually understand what’s happening. And the entire process finally moves into the 21st century. The future of mortgage isn’t coming—it’s here.

P.S. If you’re still manually underwriting loans, ask yourself: Will my business survive the next 5 years?
